title: Treacle tart
subtitle: Golden syrup and breadcrumb filling in a shortcrust pastry case
excerpt: Treacle tart is one of the most distinctly British pastry fillings: golden syrup loosened with cream and lemon juice, thickened with fine white breadcrumbs, baked in a shortcrust case until the filling is just set. Served warm with clotted cream or crème fraîche.
sourceNotes: Treacle tart as a British pastry filled with golden syrup and breadcrumbs is documented from the late nineteenth century, following the commercial introduction of Lyle's golden syrup in 1883. Mrs Beeton's later editions (post-1883 revisions, widely reproduced) include similar golden syrup tart fillings. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), records treacle tart variants from across British regional cookery. The combination of golden syrup, breadcrumbs, and lemon juice in a shortcrust case is the canonical form and is fully public-domain, reproduced in domestic baking references throughout the twentieth century.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the day before and reheat at 160°C fan for 10 minutes before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 30 to 35 minutes until filling is golden and just set with a slight wobble

glossaryTerms.definition: Treacle tart filling is done when the edges are firm and the centre has a slight wobble, like a just-set jelly. The breadcrumbs absorb the syrup and set the filling as the tart cools; a wobble in the oven becomes a firm, barely yielding texture at room temperature. If the filling is completely still in the oven, it will be too firm when cold. The bake time is 30 to 35 minutes at 180°C fan; check at 30 minutes and look for the edge-firm, centre-wobble signal.

content.text: Treacle tart has a deceptively short ingredient list. The quality of the golden syrup does the work; do not substitute it. The lemon juice is essential — it cuts the sweetness and prevents the filling from becoming cloying.

items.prepNote: fine white breadcrumbs — fresh from 2 to 3 slices, or dried

content.text: Roll the {{shortcrust-pastry}} to about 3 mm thickness on a lightly floured surface. Lift over the rolling pin and drape into the 23 cm tart tin. Press into the corners and trim the overhang. Prick the base all over with a fork. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 180°C fan.
Warm the {{golden-syrup}} gently in a saucepan until it loosens. Remove from the heat and stir in the {{double-cream}}, the juice of the {{lemon}}, and the {{breadcrumbs-dried}}. Stir to combine.
Brush the pastry rim with the beaten {{eggs}}. Pour the filling into the chilled case. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes. The
is a firm edge with a slight wobble in the centre. Leave to cool in the tin for 30 minutes; the filling will set as it cools. Serve warm with clotted cream or crème fraîche.
